summary.title: New Threat Model
summary.description:
This system is an AWS-based content-research and AI document-processing platform called “CRP” [1]. It is designed to accept traffic through a public ingress layer, protected by a WAF and load balancer, and route requests into backend application services running on ECS Fargate [1].

At the application layer, the platform uses an ECS cluster named `crp-platform` with several containerized services [1]:
- `backend-orchestrator` as the main coordination/API service [1]
- `extraction-service` for document extraction tasks [1]
- `research-agent` for research-related processing [1]
- `ingestion-worker` for ingestion/background job handling [1]

The backend orchestrates work across the internal services, forming a pipeline where incoming requests trigger extraction, research, and worker processes [1].

For storage, the platform uses multiple AWS-managed data services:
- PostgreSQL RDS (`crp-case-db`) for structured case data, with storage encryption enabled [1]
- S3 bucket (`crp-ingested-documents`) for document storage, protected with KMS-backed server-side encryption [1]
- AWS Secrets Manager (`crp/app-secrets`) for sensitive application secrets, also protected with KMS [1]

The architecture also integrates private AI capabilities through VPC endpoints, specifically for services like Textract and Bedrock, which suggests support for OCR and LLM-based document analysis without exposing those calls over the public internet [1].

In short, this is a cloud-native, microservice-style AWS platform for ingesting, processing, researching, and storing documents and case data, with encrypted storage, secret management, and private AI-service integration [1].
